title: cornus × dunbarii
intro: Cornus × dunbarii, also known as the Gray Dogwood or Red Osier Dogwood, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that is a hybrid of Cornus sericea and Cornus kousa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, diarrhea, and inflammation, due to its high content of flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Cornus × dunbarii include its potential to improve cardiovascular health, reduce oxidative stress, and exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. Cornus × dunbarii can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
